title: change `language_detection_threshold` default value
body: the current value of `language_detection_threshold` means that `language_detection_segments` will be 1 regardless of the user set value, the new value will ensure that `language_detection_segments` is respected

files_modified.patch:
@@ -692,7 +692,7 @@ def transcribe(
         clip_timestamps: Union[str, List[float]] = "0",
         hallucination_silence_threshold: Optional[float] = None,
         hotwords: Optional[str] = None,
-        language_detection_threshold: Optional[float] = None,
+        language_detection_threshold: Optional[float] = 0.5,
         language_detection_segments: int = 1,
     ) -> Tuple[Iterable[Segment], TranscriptionInfo]:
         """Transcribes an input file.
@@ -882,10 +882,7 @@ def transcribe(
                     ]
                     # Get top language token and probability
                     language, language_probability = all_language_probs[0]
-                    if (
-                        language_detection_threshold is None
-                        or language_probability > language_detection_threshold
-                    ):
+                    if language_probability > language_detection_threshold:
                         break
                     detected_language_info.setdefault(language, []).append(
                         language_probability

steps.operation: Change the default `language_detection_threshold` parameter to `0.5` and simplify the early-exit condition to always compare the detected language probability against this threshold.
steps.reason: With `language_detection_threshold=None`, the loop breaks unconditionally on the first segment (because the condition treated `None` as always passing), effectively forcing `language_detection_segments` to behave as 1 regardless of the user-provided value. Setting a concrete default (0.5) and removing the `None` bypass ensures the loop only breaks early when confidence is sufficiently high, otherwise it continues evaluating up to `language_detection_segments` segments.
steps.side_effects: Language detection may run for more segments by default (potentially higher latency) when early segments have low confidence; any callers/tests relying on the previous behavior (immediate break when threshold was unset/None) may see different detected language outcomes or performance characteristics.

step_plan.summary: Adjusts `language_detection_threshold` to default to 0.5 and removes the special-case `None` logic so that `language_detection_segments` is properly respected during language detection.
